description: Fleet/ride-hail drivers charge at home or depot (20%). Rest is public DC.
Fleet/ride-hail drivers use public DC charging (80%).
Each DC fast charger serves ~7 vehicles/day (45 min charge over 16 hrs operating)
Each AC charger serves ~2.5 vehicles/day (6-8 hr charge over 16 hrs operating)
Each battery swap station serves ~120 vehicles/day (~3 min avg visit including walk)
Above 70% utilisation considered congested (healthy cap for capacity calc)
Below 30% utilisation = Low contention
Above 60% utilisation = High contention
Above 85% utilisation = Critical bottleneck
ICE vehicle range per full tank used to estimate refuel frequency
Time per ICE refuel stop including travel to station
Operating days per year for fleet/taxi use cases
Annual battery capacity degradation (2%/yr affects EV efficiency)
Financing cost shortcut: price × interest_rate × years × 0.55
Exponential residual value decay rate (exp(-0.25 × year))
Insurance cost floor as ratio of year-1 insurance (min 70%)
Annual insurance cost reduction rate (3%/yr)
Annual maintenance cost growth rate (5%/yr)
EV subsidy cannot reduce price below 50% of original price
Grid connection fee per DC charger for car fleets
Grid connection fee per DC charger for motorbike fleets
One-time EV onboarding and training cost per driver
DC charger annual maintenance cost as % of capex (6%)
AC charger annual maintenance cost as % of capex (4%)
EV fleet management overhead per car per year
EV fleet management overhead per motorbike per year
Higher financing cost for EV loans vs ICE (2% extra due to resale uncertainty)
Sensitivity analysis tests each variable at +20% of current value
Motorbikes need ~70% charge mid-shift (not full charge)
Battery swap station visit time including walk (~5 min)
